steps.narrate: Verdan Market — the Belt's largest trading hub, and the Bureau's most tolerated black eye.
It sprawled across three old canyons, stitched together with rope-bridges and smoke and the low hum of too many voices at once.
Somewhere down there, Malet kept a desk. And Malet, if rumor held, kept debts — including whatever was owed to the name Arrel no longer wanted to say aloud.

steps.speaker: Elia
steps.text: Stay close. This place eats travelers.

steps.speaker: Arrel
steps.text: I'll stay close. You keep your hood up.

steps.speaker: Arrel
steps.text: Worried about what someone might recognize.

steps.narrate: Below them, the market opened its mouth.
A guard stopped them at the southern stairs. Bureau insignia. Too alert for a Belt posting.

steps.speaker: Guard
steps.text: Names. Purpose. Papers if you have them.

choice.text: Tell him we're hunters tracking rogue Husks.
Surface a memory of a bribe. Pay him off with what he wants to hear.

steps.narrate: Either way, the market swallowed them whole.
